description_long: This object resembles a traditional Japanese torii gate, but with a modern, electronic twist. It features two robust cylindrical pillars made of a dark reddish-brown material, capped with black bases, supporting a thick, gently curved black top beam. The crossbeam beneath the top is also dark red, adding structural and visual balance. The surface appears smooth and polished, suggesting a lacquered or coated finish. There are no visible screens, buttons, or control panels, indicating that any electronic components are likely internal or subtly integrated. The overall design is both decorative and functional, suitable for urban parks or themed public spaces.
description: A modern, torii-inspired electronic structure with dark red pillars and a curved black top beam, featuring a smooth, polished finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both cylindrical pillars, the curved black top beam, and the red crossbeam.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the structure and the thickness of the top beam.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying both pillars and the crossbeam.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the curvature of the top beam.
